perturbation.original_text: The purchase price shall be two times Agency's commissions on business produced directly by Agency during the last 12 full months preceding the termination date.
perturbation.changed_text: The purchase price shall be two times Agency's commissions on business produced directly by Agency during the last 12 full months preceding the termination date, unless MICOA determines, at its sole discretion, that a different valuation method is more appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds uncertainty to the calculation of the purchase price of expirations upon termination. While the original text specifies a clear calculation (two times Agency's commissions), the added clause allows MICOA to unilaterally decide on a 'different valuation method,' contradicting the previously defined method and creating ambiguity about the actual purchase price. The location that is being contradicted is still in Section D, as the method of the valuation is being changed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event this Agreement is terminated for any reason, MICOA agrees to purchase from Agency, and Agency agrees to sell to MICOA Agency's ownership interest in the expirations for the MICOA insurance issued pursuant to this Agreement. The purchase price shall be two times Agency's commissions on business produced directly by Agency during the last 12 full months preceding the termination date, unless MICOA determines, at its sole discretion, that a different valuation method is more appropriate. The purchase shall be completed within 60 calendar days after the termination date. In return for this payment, for a two-year period following the termination date, Agency will not directly or indirectly sell any professional liability insurance to any individuals or entities who were MICOA insureds in Nevada at the time of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause that allows MICOA to unilaterally decide on a 'different valuation method,' contradicting the previously defined method of calculating the purchase price (two times Agency's commissions) in the original text and in the contradicted text. This introduces uncertainty and ambiguity regarding the actual purchase price, creating a direct conflict and making the contract unenforceable as it relates to the valuation.

perturbation.original_text: Agency may solicit subagencies for appointment, subject to MICOA's prior written approval of each subagency following disclosure to and review by MICOA of information requested by MICOA for each proposed subagency.
perturbation.changed_text: Agency may solicit subagencies for appointment at its sole discretion, without MICOA's approval or notification. MICOA retains the right to refuse or nullify all subagency agreements.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original approval process for subagencies. Originally, MICOA's prior written approval was required. Now, the Agency can solicit subagencies without MICOA's approval. The addition of 'MICOA retains the right to refuse or nullify all subagency agreements' creates a conflict. The original section stipulates that Agency has to get the MICOA's approval, this new section allows Agency to solicit without it, but then also state MICOA can negate it. It is contradicting the obligation being made to Agency in section A.8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Agency may solicit subagencies for appointment at its sole discretion, without MICOA's approval or notification. MICOA retains the right to refuse or nullify all subagency agreements. All such appointments by Agent shall stipulate that MICOA may terminate the subagency at any time without cause upon at least 90 days notice and that the subagency shall comply with all MICOA requirements and duties owed MICOA by Agency concerning solicitation, communications, and service to insureds. Subagencies shall also be required to submit all proposals immediately and directly to MICOA.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states the 'Agency may solicit subagencies for appointment at its sole discretion, without MICOA's approval or notification'. This directly conflicts with the original text that states 'Agency may solicit subagencies for appointment, subject to MICOA's prior written approval'. Although the changed text states 'MICOA retains the right to refuse or nullify all subagency agreements' the Agency now does not need to seek approval, but MICOA can still nullify the decision. If MICOA can nullify these agreements, it essentially retains veto power. This still does not mean the Agency is obligated to get approval, which is a conflict to the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Agency shall refrain from admitting or denying liability on the part of the company in connection with any claim or lawsuit.
perturbation.changed_text: Agency shall, at its discretion, admit or deny liability on the part of the company in connection with any claim or lawsuit, except when expressly prohibited by MICOA in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly prohibits the Agency from admitting or denying liability. The modified clause now allows the Agency to do so at its discretion, unless MICOA specifically prohibits it in writing. This introduces a direct conflict and ambiguity regarding the Agency's authority in handling claims and potential legal ramifications for MICOA depending on when and whether MICOA sends such written prohibitions. This is contradiction the entire first section, as it is an authority being granted to Agency now that was previously prohibited.
perturbation.contradicted_text: To promptly report all claims and losses of which the Agency has knowledge and properly notify MICOA when the Agency receives notice of the commencement of any related legal action. Agency shall, at its discretion, admit or deny liability on the part of the company in connection with any claim or lawsuit, except when expressly prohibited by MICOA in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly forbids the Agency from admitting or denying liability. The changed text grants the Agency the discretion to do so unless MICOA expressly prohibits it in writing. This directly contradicts the prior restriction, creating uncertainty and a potential conflict in handling claims. The contradicted text reinforces the Agency's reporting duties. The changed clause introduces a loophole that allows the Agency to admit or deny liability unless expressly told not to, causing direct contradiction.
